Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log with three cases The activities are RA Receive Application VF Validate Form AE Assess Eligibility RMI Request Missing Info AD Approval Decision SC Send Confirmation AC Archive Case Observing the cases Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We see that the main sequence is RA VF AE then optionally a loop that involves RMI and then VF again then AD SC AC In case 1 there is one RMI and then VF again In case 2 there is no RMI In case 3 there are two RMIs in a row and then VF again Note that after the loop if it occurs we always have AD SC AC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So we can think of the process as 1 Start with RA 2 Then VF 3 Then AE 4 Then optionally a loop that involves RMI and then VF again

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Looking at the timestamps in case 3 AE at 0910 RMI at 0925 RMI at 0945 VF at 1020 It seems that after the first RMI we might have another RMI without going through VF

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the behavior is after AE we may have a sequence of one or more RMIs and then we must have VF again before moving to AD

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can use a loop that does RMI and then either exits or does another RMI But then we would not have VF until after the loop So one possible model is We have a loop that repeats RMI The loop is do RMI then either exit or do RMI again Then after the loop we do VF

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So we need to have a choice either skip the loop and go directly to AD or enter the loop which is RMI one or more times and then do VF and then AD So we can model it as After AE we have an XOR X between two branches Branch 1 AD SC AC Branch 2 RMI loop then VF then AD SC AC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The loop RMI tau would mean do RMI then either exit or do tau and then do RMI again This allows for one or more RMIs

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets check with the cases Case 1 with one RMI RA VF AE choose branch2 do RMI and exit the loop VF AD SC AC Case 2 no RMI RA VF AE choose branch1 AD SC AC Case 3 two RMIs RA VF AE choose branch2 do RMI then instead of exiting do tau and then RMI again then exit the loop VF AD SC AC This matches

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: We can also write it without the extra sequence nodes by combining the sequences But the tree above is clear

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Alternatively we can write root RA VF AE X branch1 branch2 branch1 AD SC AC branch2 RMI tau VF AD SC AC

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Alternatively we can think of the process as after AE we do a loop that might include RMI and then VF but then we always do AD SC AC So another way is RA VF AE RMI VF tau AD SC AC But this would mean after AE we do a loop and the loop operator always executes the first child at least once So that does not work for case 2 Therefore the XOR model is the one that fits
